mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in CPIAUCSL signals disinflationary pressure that reduces uncertainty about future real discount rates, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If month-over-month CPIAUCSL declines are followed by negative 20d SPX returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 6.0%, it signals deteriorating labor demand that reduces household income expectations and compresses corporate earnings forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE > 6.0% episodes are followed by positive 60d SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) capture a coordinated demand-supply expansion that amplifies corporate profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If joint increases in RSAFS and INDPRO fail to produce positive 5d SPX returns in at least 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the federal funds rate is below 0.5%, an acceleration in consumer price inflation captured by month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases signals successful reflation that lifts nominal corporate revenues and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL increases during FEDFUNDS < 0.5% periods are followed by negative 60d SPX returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) signals improving manufacturing utilization and operating leverage that boosts earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO acceleration fails to produce positive 5d SPX returns in at least 55% of months over a 2-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,000 thousand units, it signals a contraction in residential investment that reduces construction activity and durable goods demand, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If HOUST < 1000 episodes are followed by negative 20d SPX returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of falling unemployment (UNRATE month-over-month decrease) and rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS month-over-month increase) signals a robust economy where monetary tightening is absorbed without labor market distress, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If joint falling UNRATE and rising FEDFUNDS episodes fail to produce positive 60d SPX returns in at least 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals broad aggregate demand expansion that supports top-line revenue growth across sectors, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If positive GDPC1 growth prints are followed by negative 5d SPX returns in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window, reject.
